content.text: Without a self-service provisioning layer, every new GenAI service must wait for a platform engineer to manually create API credentials, set quota limits, and write secrets into the appropriate vault path. At scale this becomes a bottleneck: a week's wait for a credential blocks a sprint. The self-service provisioning system lets teams request API keys with scoped quotas through a Backstage form, receive credentials injected into their Kubernetes pods within minutes, and have those credentials automatically rotated and metered by the LLM gateway.
Why Self-Service Matters at Scale
Manual provisioning problem
Self-service solution
Platform engineer required for every credential request
Backstage form triggers fully automated workflow; no human in the loop for standard-tier requests
Keys shared across services (no per-service isolation)
Each service gets a unique scoped key; compromise is contained to one service
Quota limits set once and never reviewed
TTL-based quotas auto-expire; teams must renew and can adjust via self-service
No audit trail linking a key to its requesting team
Every key creation emits a structured audit event to the SIEM with requester identity
Key rotation requires manual coordination
Vault dynamic secret engine rotates keys automatically on TTL expiry
Provisioning Workflow

The workflow is triggered through a Backstage software template. Teams fill in a form; the scaffolder action calls the provisioning service API, which orchestrates credential creation across Vault and the LLM gateway.
Step 1: Backstage Template Form

# backstage-template-api-key/template.yaml
apiVersion: scaffolder.backstage.io/v1beta3
kind: Template
metadata:
  name: provision-llm-api-key
  title: Provision LLM API Key
spec:
  owner: group:platform-ai
  type:  infrastructure
  parameters:
    - title: Service Details
      properties:
        service_name:
          title: Backstage component name
          type: string
          description: Must match an existing catalog component
        model_tier:
          title: Model tier
          type: string
          enum: [standard, premium]
          default: standard
        daily_budget_usd:
          title: Daily spend budget (USD)
          type: number
          minimum: 1
          maximum: 500
          default: 20
        ttl_days:
          title: Credential TTL (days)
          type: integer
          minimum: 30
          maximum: 365
          default: 90
        justification:
          title: Business justification (required for premium tier)
          type: string
  steps:
    - id: provision
      name: Provision credentials
      action: genai:provision-api-key
      input:
        service_name:     ${{ parameters.service_name }}
        model_tier:       ${{ parameters.model_tier }}
        daily_budget_usd: ${{ parameters.daily_budget_usd }}
        ttl_days:         ${{ parameters.ttl_days }}
        requester:        ${{ user.entity.metadata.name }}
  output:
    links:
      - title: View credentials in Vault
        url: ${{ steps.provision.output.vault_path_url }}
      - title: View quota in gateway
        url: ${{ steps.provision.output.gateway_quota_url }}
Step 2: Provisioning Service API
The Backstage scaffolder action calls the provisioning service's REST API. The service enforces the approval policy before touching Vault or the gateway.

from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Depends
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
import secrets, hashlib

app = FastAPI()

APPROVAL_THRESHOLDS = {
    "standard": {"max_daily_usd": 50,  "requires_approval": False},
    "premium":  {"max_daily_usd": 500, "requires_approval": True},
}

class ProvisionRequest(BaseModel):
    service_name:     str
    model_tier:       str  = Field(..., pattern="^(standard|premium)$")
    daily_budget_usd: float = Field(..., gt=0, le=500)
    ttl_days:         int  = Field(..., ge=30, le=365)
    requester:        str

class ProvisionResponse(BaseModel):
    vault_path:      str
    gateway_key_id:  str
    daily_budget_usd:float
    expires_at:      str

@app.post("/api/provision-key", response_model=ProvisionResponse)
async def provision_key(
    req: ProvisionRequest,
    claims: dict = Depends(require_scope("platform:provision")),
):
    # Validate service exists in catalog
    component = await catalog_client.get_component(req.service_name)
    if not component:
        raise HTTPException(404, f"Component '{req.service_name}' not found in catalog")

    # Enforce tier-based approval policy
    policy = APPROVAL_THRESHOLDS[req.model_tier]
    if req.daily_budget_usd > policy["max_daily_usd"]:
        raise HTTPException(403, f"Budget ${req.daily_budget_usd} exceeds self-service limit for {req.model_tier} tier")
    if policy["requires_approval"]:
        ticket_id = await create_approval_ticket(req)
        raise HTTPException(202, f"Premium tier requires approval. Ticket: {ticket_id}")

    # Create credentials
    vault_path, key_value = await vault_create_key(req)
    gateway_key_id        = await gateway_register_key(req, key_value)

    # Emit audit event
    await emit_audit("provision-api-key", req.service_name,
                     requester=req.requester, tier=req.model_tier)

    return ProvisionResponse(
        vault_path=vault_path,
        gateway_key_id=gateway_key_id,
        daily_budget_usd=req.daily_budget_usd,
        expires_at=compute_expiry(req.ttl_days),
    )
Step 3: Vault Dynamic Secrets

Vault's KV v2 engine stores the key with a metadata TTL. The Vault agent sidecar injector automatically mounts the secret into the pod's filesystem and refreshes it before expiry — the service code never handles key rotation explicitly.

import hvac, secrets, hashlib
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

async def vault_create_key(req: ProvisionRequest) -> tuple[str, str]:
    client = hvac.Client()   # VAULT_ADDR + VAULT_TOKEN from env

    # Generate a cryptographically random key
    raw_key  = secrets.token_urlsafe(40)
    key_hash = hashlib.sha256(raw_key.encode()).hexdigest()[:12]
    key_id   = f"genai-{req.service_name}-{key_hash}"

    vault_path = f"genai/api-keys/{req.service_name}"
    expiry     = datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=req.ttl_days)

    client.secrets.kv.v2.create_or_update_secret(
        path=vault_path,
        secret={
            "key":             raw_key,
            "key_id":          key_id,
            "service_name":    req.service_name,
            "model_tier":      req.model_tier,
            "daily_budget_usd":str(req.daily_budget_usd),
            "expires_at":      expiry.isoformat(),
        },
        cas=0,   # must not already exist
    )

    # Set Vault metadata TTL for auto-deletion after key expires
    client.secrets.kv.v2.update_metadata(
        path=vault_path,
        delete_version_after=f"{req.ttl_days * 24}h",
    )
    return vault_path, raw_key

# Pod annotation to auto-inject:
# vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject: "true"
# vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject-secret-llm-key: "genai/api-keys/my-service"
# vault.hashicorp.com/role: "genai-service"
Quota Enforcement in the LLM Gateway
The LLM gateway enforces the daily budget using a token-bucket counter stored in Redis. When a service's daily spend reaches its budget, the gateway returns HTTP 429 with a
Retry-After
header pointing to the next UTC midnight reset.

import redis.asyncio as redis
from datetime import datetime, timezone

r = redis.Redis(host="redis.internal", decode_responses=True)

async def check_quota(key_id: str, estimated_cost_usd: float) -> None:
    today   = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    counter = f"quota:{key_id}:{today}"

    # Atomic increment and check
    pipe = r.pipeline()
    pipe.incrbyfloat(counter, estimated_cost_usd)
    pipe.expire(counter, 86_400)   # expire at end of day (safety net)
    results = await pipe.execute()

    spent = float(results[0])
    budget= await r.hget(f"key_meta:{key_id}", "daily_budget_usd")
    if budget and spent > float(budget):
        raise QuotaExceeded(
            f"Daily budget ${budget} exhausted (spent ${spent:.4f}). "
            "Resets at UTC midnight."
        )

class QuotaExceeded(Exception): pass

# Gateway middleware usage:
# Before forwarding to LLM provider, call check_quota with estimated token cost
# After receiving the response, update with actual token cost
Self-Service Quota Increase Requests

Teams that routinely hit their quota can request an increase through the same Backstage form. Requests up to 2× the current budget with a written justification are auto-approved; larger increases require the team's engineering manager and the platform team's cost governance review.
Increase magnitude
Approval path
SLA
Up to 2× current budget
Auto-approved; justification field required but not reviewed
Immediate (within 5 minutes)
2×–5× current budget
Engineering manager approval via Slack workflow
Within 1 business day
5×+ or premium tier initial request
Engineering manager + platform cost governance review
Within 3 business days
Emergency budget increase (production incident)
On-call platform engineer via PagerDuty escalation
Within 30 minutes
Key Rotation and Emergency Revocation

Keys rotate automatically when their Vault TTL expires. The Vault agent sidecar handles re-injection with zero pod restarts required. Emergency revocation — when a key is suspected to be compromised — triggers a three-step response:
Revoke the key in Vault immediately (Vault's lease revocation propagates within 1 second).
Remove the key from the LLM gateway's allowlist (gateway polls Vault every 30 seconds; force-sync available via platform API).
Provision a replacement key via the self-service form; the new key appears in the pod within 60 seconds via the Vault agent sidecar.

# Emergency revocation — run by platform on-call engineer
# 1. Revoke in Vault
vault kv delete genai/api-keys/compromised-service

# 2. Force-sync the LLM gateway allowlist
curl -X POST https://llm-gateway.internal/admin/sync-keys \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $GATEWAY_ADMIN_TOKEN"

# 3. Verify key is rejected
curl -X POST https://llm-gateway.internal/v1/complete \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $COMPROMISED_KEY" \
  -d '{"model":"gpt-4o-mini","messages":[]}' | jq .error
# → {"error": "invalid_api_key"}
Kubernetes Integration — Vault Agent Sidecar

The Vault agent sidecar injector handles secret delivery without any application-level secret management code. Annotate the pod spec and the sidecar fetches, renders, and refreshes the secret automatically.

# kubernetes/deployment-patch.yaml
spec:
  template:
    metadata:
      annotations:
        vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject:                         "true"
        vault.hashicorp.com/role:                                 "genai-service"
        # Inject the LLM API key as a file at /vault/secrets/llm-key
        vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject-secret-llm-key:         "genai/api-keys/my-service"
        # Render into a shell-sourceable format
        vault.hashicorp.com/agent-inject-template-llm-key: |
          {{- with secret "genai/api-keys/my-service" -}}
          export LLM_API_KEY="{{ .Data.data.key }}"
          export LLM_KEY_EXPIRES="{{ .Data.data.expires_at }}"
          {{- end }}
        # Renew 24 hours before expiry
        vault.hashicorp.com/agent-pre-populate-only:             "false"
        vault.hashicorp.com/secret-volume-path-llm-key:          "/vault/secrets"
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: my-genai-service  # must bind to the genai-service Vault role
      containers:
        - name: app
          env:
            - name: LLM_API_KEY
              valueFrom:
                secretKeyRef:   # fallback: populated by init container from /vault/secrets/llm-key
                  name: llm-key-bootstrap
                  key: key
The Vault role
genai-service
is bound to the Kubernetes service account via Vault's Kubernetes auth method. The role policy grants read-only access to
genai/api-keys/*
so a compromised sidecar can only read keys, not create or delete them.

# vault-policy: genai-service-read
path "genai/api-keys/*" {
  capabilities = ["read"]
}

# Vault Kubernetes auth role
vault write auth/kubernetes/role/genai-service \
  bound_service_account_names="my-genai-service" \
  bound_service_account_namespaces="production" \
  policies="genai-service-read" \
  ttl="1h"
Monitoring Quota Spend and Alerting
The LLM gateway exposes per-key spend metrics to Prometheus. Teams can add a Grafana panel to their service dashboard and configure alerts when daily spend approaches the budget limit.

# Prometheus alerting rules for quota spend
groups:
  - name: genai.quota
    rules:
      - alert: QuotaAbove80Pct
        expr: |
          genai_key_daily_spend_usd
          / on(key_id) genai_key_daily_budget_usd
          > 0.80
        for: 5m
        labels:
          severity: warning
          team: "{{ $labels.owner_team }}"
        annotations:
          summary: "LLM key {{ $labels.key_id }} at {{ $value | humanizePercentage }} of daily budget"
          description: "Service {{ $labels.service_name }} will be throttled if spend continues at this rate."

      - alert: QuotaExhausted
        expr: |
          genai_key_daily_spend_usd
          >= on(key_id) genai_key_daily_budget_usd
        for: 1m
        labels:
          severity: critical
          team: "{{ $labels.owner_team }}"
        annotations:
          summary: "LLM key {{ $labels.key_id }} quota exhausted — requests are blocked"
          runbook_url: "https://wiki.internal/genai/runbooks/quota-exhausted"
Metric name
Type
Labels
Description
genai_key_daily_spend_usd
Gauge
key_id, service_name, model_tier
Running daily spend for this key (resets UTC midnight)
genai_key_daily_budget_usd
Gauge
key_id, service_name
Configured daily budget ceiling
genai_key_request_total
Counter
key_id, model, status
Total requests routed through this key
genai_key_tokens_total
Counter
key_id, model, direction
Cumulative tokens (input/output) for cost attribution
genai_key_ttl_days
Gauge
key_id, service_name
Days remaining before key TTL expires
Common Pitfalls
Pitfall
Symptom
Fix
Setting TTL too short for batch jobs
Pod restarts unexpectedly mid-job as key TTL expires during long-running workload
Set TTL ≥ 2× the longest expected job duration; use Vault lease renewal for batch workloads
Sharing one key across multiple services
Quota exhausted by one high-traffic service blocks all others sharing the key
Provision one key per Backstage component; use per-service quota allocation
Not setting cas=0 on first write
Provisioning service silently overwrites an existing key, invalidating the old one mid-request
Always use cas=0 (check-and-set) on create; treat conflict as an error and surface it to the requester
Relying on Vault metadata TTL for security
Vault auto-deletes the secret path after TTL, but gateway continues honoring the key until its allowlist refreshes
Pair Vault TTL with gateway key expiry: provision service must write key expiry to both Vault metadata and gateway
Over-provisioning premium tier by default
Cost governance loses visibility; budget reviews find every service on premium even for low-volume workloads
Default new requests to standard tier; require PM sign-off in the justification field for premium initial requests
Summary

Self-service API key and quota provisioning eliminates the manual bottleneck that blocks GenAI teams from reaching production. The Backstage template form, provisioning service API, Vault dynamic secrets, and LLM gateway quota enforcement work together so that a team can go from zero credentials to a metered, automatically-rotating key injected into their pod in under five minutes. The approval policy — auto-approve for standard-tier requests under the budget cap, escalate for premium or high-budget requests — keeps costs under control without creating unnecessary friction. Emergency revocation and forced gateway sync ensure that a compromised key can be neutralised and replaced before meaningful damage occurs.
